title: Talbot-Lau Grating Interferometry (X-ray phase-contrast imaging)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (genesis fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical Talbot-Lau literature (Pfeiffer, Weitkamp, David, Momose) for polychromatic X-ray phase imaging with partially-coherent lab sources.

E.description: Three X-ray gratings (G0 source grating, G1 phase grating, G2 analyzer grating) encode phase and small-angle scattering as a spatially modulated moire pattern. Phase stepping (typically 8 steps) of one grating recovers three contrast images per pixel: absorption (A), differential phase (phi), and dark-field (visibility V).
E.forward_model: I_k(x,y) = a_0(x,y) + a_1(x,y) * cos(2*pi*k/K + phi(x,y)); Fourier analysis over k yields (A, phi, V)
E.world_state_x: Triplet (A(x,y), phi(x,y), V(x,y)) for absorption, differential phase, dark-field
E.observation_y: K phase-stepped intensity frames I_k(x,y), k = 1..K (polychromatic X-ray counting)
physical_parameters_theta: Grating periods p_0, p_1, p_2 and duty cycles
Inter-grating distances d_01, d_12 (Talbot self-imaging condition)
mean energy E_mean and spectrum width (polychromatic source)
source coherence length (set by G0 slit width)

W.regime: Well-conditioned linear Fourier extraction of three contrast channels from K phase-stepped frames (K >= 3 required; K = 8 typical for noise averaging). Stability dominated by grating alignment and vibration; dark-field channel degrades when visibility V < 0.15.

C.solver_class: Fourier demodulation (analytical), weighted least-squares, TV-regularized ADMM for A/phi/V fusion, learned (TalbotNet)
C.error_bound: sigma_phi = sqrt(2) / (sqrt(K) * V * sqrt(photon_count)); dark-field noise ~ 1/sqrt(photon_count); bias from detrending cross-talk at V < 0.15
C.complexity: O(K * H * W) for Fourier extraction
